channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump meets with Macron, Zelenskyy ahead of Notre Dame reopening ceremony in Paris
item.description: President-elect Trump visited with French President Emmanuel Macron and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy ahead of the Notre Dame reopening ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says US shouldn't intervene in Syria as opposition forces gain ground
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Saturday that the United States should avoid engaging militarily in Syria amid an opposition offensive that has reached the capital's suburbs, declaring in a social media post, "THIS IS NOT OUR FIGHT.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Speaker’s Lobby: Thermonuclear Fracas
item.description: Despite the intent to find solutions following the assassination attempt of President-elect Trump back in July, bipartisan lawmakers got into a heated exchange.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Election Day has long passed. In some states, legislatures are working to undermine the results
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — While the election was over a month ago, voters in some parts of the country are discovering that having their say at the ballot box is not necessarily the final word. Lawmakers in several states have already initiated or indicated plans to alter or nullify certain results.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mayoral hopeful Michael Blake discusses vision for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – As we approach 2025, the new year marks a new election cycle. Political consultant Michael Blake joins PIX on Politics to discuss his decision to join the long list of Democrats competing with Mayor Eric Adams in next year’s mayoral primary.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Congressman Torres blasts crime in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The cold-blooded shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in the heart of Manhattan shocked New Yorkers, including Congressman Ritchie Torres, leaving the lawmaker to pose the question: “What is happening in New York City?” Torres joined PIX on Politics to discuss city crim
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: How China's cyberespionage has changed
item.description: China's cyberespionage has evolved over the last several decades from targeting entities such as government departments to the public tech sector.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Military leaders are rattled by a list of 'woke' officers that a group urges Hegseth to fire
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Military leaders are rattled by a list of “woke” senior officers that a conservative group urged Pete Hegseth to dismiss for promoting diversity in the ranks if he is confirmed to lead the Pentagon.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance tells residents in hurricane-stricken North Carolina that they haven't been forgotten
item.description: FAIRVIEW, N.C. (AP) — Vice President-elect JD Vance on Friday assured residents of western North Carolina still cleaning up from Hurricane Helene that they haven't been forgotten as he surveyed storm wreckage and talked to first responders in one of his first public appearances since the election.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and GARY ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mother of Austin Tice, journalist missing in Syria, says new information proves her son is alive
item.description: The mother of Austin Tice, an American journalist missing in Syria for more than a decade, says she's confident that her son is alive.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: GOP senator announces 'DOGE Acts' to back Musk, Ramaswamy government cost-cutting objectives
item.description: Blackburn announced she will be introducing the "DOGE Acts," following meetings with Musk and Ramaswamy on Capitol Hill on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AOC launches bid for top Democratic role on House Oversight Committee
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, D-N.Y., announced she is running for the top Democratic position on the House Oversight Committee next Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden attends national Christmas tree lighting on windy Washington night
item.description: President Biden attended the national Christmas tree lighting as he prepares to depart the White House in January as the 46th President of the United States of America.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The first rioter tried on Jan. 6 charges gets reduced prison sentence after Supreme Court decision
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Texas man who was the first rioter to go on trial for the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol was resentenced on Friday to nearly seven years in prison after he delivered an angry, profane rant to the judge who agreed to modestly reduce his original sentence.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Newsom visits border, slams Trump on planned deportations, tariffs
item.description: "No state will be more impacted by that, no region will be impacted by that, small businesses that will lose customers, jobs that will be lost, inflationary impacts on every single one of you," said Newsom.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Biden EPA makes first-ever climate change arrest
item.description: President Biden's Environmental Protection Agency enforcement report reveals increased penalties for polluters, including the first-ever climate-change related arrest.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump talks up his transition and election victory as he receives Fox Nation award
item.description: GREENVALE, N.Y. (AP) — Not yet even in office, Donald Trump said Thursday he's already accomplished more than President Joe Biden did in his entire term, an outsized claim by the president-elect just a month out from the election.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former national security officials urge caution on Gabbard
item.description: A group of nearly 100 former national security officials urged the Senate to “carefully scrutinize” former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s nominee to serve as director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Biden lights National Christmas tree for last time as president
item.description: President Joe Biden presided over the lighting of the National Christmas tree for his final time as commander-in-chief on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds law requiring sale or ban of TikTok in the US
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Friday unanimously upheld a law that could lead to a ban on TikTok as soon as next month, handing a resounding defeat to the popular social media platform as it fights for its survival in the U.S. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump names David Sacks as AI, crypto czar
item.description: President-elect Trump said Thursday that venture capitalist David Sacks will serve as the White House artificial intelligence (AI) and cryptocurrency czar.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: NYC plan would abolish income tax for certain residents: See if you qualify
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A new proposal would abolish New York City’s personal income tax for nearly half a million qualifying residents.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Missouri abortion ban slated to lift Friday if judge allows
item.description: A judge must decide whether “targeted regulation of abortion providers” laws are also unconstitutional under Amendment 3.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Talking Sense: Coming together to solve tough problems
item.description: Mariah Levison and Rob Fersh help people find consensus on how to solve divisive issues, ranging from prison reform to what art should hang in the Minnesota Capitol. Learn how they do it in this MPR News Talking Sense conversation hosted by MPR News correspondent Catharine Richert.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis, Catharine Richert, Maja Beckstrom, and Annie Baxter, MPR News

item.title: Head of Democratic Caucus says thin House margins make attendance vital next year
item.description: The head of the House Democratic Caucus is sending an early message to his troops heading into the 119th Congress: Attendance will be crucial given the razor-tight margins in the lower chamber. “It’s important for every member to come to work and to do their job.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: International students confused, anxious about their futures under second Trump term
item.description: International students are holding their breath as President-elect Trump gears up to return to the White House. Multiple schools have put out guidance on when foreign-born students should and shouldn't go abroad to ensure they are in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Kevin McCarthy: Gaetz lied to Trump before AG nomination
item.description: Should the House Ethics Committee still release its report on Gaetz, even if he’s not going to be part of Trump’s Cabinet?
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Nadler bows out of Judiciary race, will endorse Raskin
item.description: Rep. Jerry Nadler (D-N.Y.) is ceding his position as top Democrat on the powerful Judiciary Committee amid a tough challenge from Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-Md.), — a stunning shift on a powerful committee that’s sure to play a considerable role in the oversight of the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Montana lawmakers cross the aisle to block a trans bathroom ban in the state capitol
item.description: Some Republicans joined Democrats in rejecting the measure, which would have restricted bathroom access. Rep. Zooey Zephyr, Montana's first openly transgender legislator, won a second term last month.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Inside RFK Jr.'s nonprofit's legal battles over vaccines and public health
item.description: The nonprofit Children's Health Defense that Kennedy led has filed nearly 30 federal and state lawsuits since 2020, many challenging vaccines and public health mandates.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond, MPR News

item.title: Condo deadline looms: Owners must confront years of neglect
item.description: The condo conundrum continues as the December 31st inspection deadline is only a few weeks away. State lawmakers promise action on the issue next session, but the clock is ticking.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California Assembly Speaker sets new bill limit for legislators
item.description: The California Assembly will limit the number of bills each legislature can introduce from 50 to 35, according to the house rules adopted at the start of the session on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Hegseth forced out of vets groups due to drinking, misconduct: New Yorker
item.description: The incidents — relayed in a trail of documents and corroborated to The New Yorker by the accounts of former colleagues — took place prior to Hegseth becoming a full-time Fox News TV host in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: GOP's small majority adds to challenge of tax reform
item.description: House Republicans are heading into the new Congress with a lofty set of tax reform goals, an ambitious agenda made more difficult by a razor-thin majority.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5
